<!-- Generated by the ccode-to-codex mirror sync on 2026-04-14 from input revision 9a2403fb. -->

# Conversational Migration Contract

Use this contract across the `migrate-*` skill family so the front-door UX stays
consistent even though the backend scripts differ.

## 1. Decide Whether to Route or Offer a Menu

Route directly when the user already gave both:

- an action: `assess`, `doctor`, `preview`, `migrate`, `validate`, `status`,
  `refresh`, `what next`
- a concrete target when the action needs one: skill name or agent name

Offer a menu when the request is bare or underspecified:

- `migrate to codex`
- `convert agents`
- `migration dashboard`
- `help me migrate`

## 2. Build a Tiny Awareness Summary First

Before showing a menu, give one or two sentences of repo-aware context:

- whether tracker state is available
- whether blockers already exist
- whether preview or validation is the safest next step

Then suggest the 1-3 most likely actions before presenting the full menu.

## 3. Use Structured Choice Only for Finite Action Selection

In Plan mode, use `request_user_input` when the choice is a short finite list:

- assess one
- preview one
- migrate one live
- batch assess / preflight
- validate / status / refresh

Do not use structured pickers for free-text target names. Ask for the skill or
agent name in normal conversation if it was not already provided.

## 4. Default-Mode Fallback

Outside Plan mode, render the same action set as a plain numbered menu. Accept
replies like:

- `1`
- `preview`
- `status`
- `refresh`

If the action still needs a target, ask for that target in a plain follow-up.

## 4a. Finite Queue Selection

When a workflow shows a finite queue of named items, keep the user inside that
subtree instead of bouncing back to the root menu immediately.

Use this pattern:

- recommend one next item
- allow `review next`
- allow `review <number>` for the currently displayed numbered queue only
- allow `review <name>` for exact-name selection
- allow `back` to return to the root workflow menu

Rules:

- Bare numbers stay reserved for the root menu.
- Numbered queue selection is valid only against the most recent displayed
  queue.
- Once a nativeness queue is shown, treat the displayed items as a finite
  session queue instead of recomputing the queue after each completed review.
- After a review outcome is recorded, re-render the same session queue minus
  the handled item.
- When the session queue is exhausted, show a finite done state and require
  `back` to return to the root menu.
- If the queue is truncated, remind the user that exact-name selection still
  works only for names that were shown in that session queue. Hidden items
  require backing out and reopening the queue.

## 5. Confirmation Rules

Require an explicit confirmation step before:

- any live write into `.codex/skills/`
- any live write into `.codex/agents/`
- any tracker refresh that writes `migration-state.json`
- any `--force` overwrite path

Preview, doctor, status, and validation do not require confirmation because
they are read-only or temp-output flows.

## 6. Backend Contract

When a migration backend supports structured output, prefer a stable shape:

- `mode`
- `scope`
- `summary`
- `items`
- `blockers`
- `next_actions`
- optional `preview_root`
- optional `report_path`

The skill should interpret these fields instead of scraping prose.

## 7. Family-Specific Menus

`migrate-to-codex`

- Assess one skill
- Preview one skill
- Migrate one skill live
- Review one migrated skill for nativeness
- Batch assess pending skills
- Explain blockers and statuses

`migrate-agents-to-codex`

- Assess one agent
- Preview one agent as TOML
- Migrate one agent live
- Review one migrated agent for nativeness
- Batch preflight all agents
- Explain MCP or skill blockers

`migration-dashboard`

- Status
- What should I migrate next?
- Review nativeness backlog
- Show unreviewed migrated artifacts
- Show migrated agents needing nativeness review
- Show migrated skills needing nativeness review
- Validate migrated artifacts
- Refresh tracker and show status

## 8. Failure UX

Malformed or missing tracker state is a first-class user-facing case:

- explain briefly what failed
- state whether the rest of the flow can continue
- offer the repair action

Do not turn tracker failure into a generic stack-trace experience.
